relations.note: Reading-order hierarchy guidance builds on the defined email type scale.
Type scale line-heights all meet the 150% body minimum.
Email stack implements the Nunito Sans role for digital.

quotes: **Agenda** (Adobe font)
**Nunito Sans** (Google font)
*Agenda may be used as live text in digital for the campaign headline only*
Headlines & headers | **Agenda** (Adobe font)
*Agenda may be used as live text in digital for the campaign headline only*
Body copy | **Nunito Sans**
Available Agenda weights: Light, Regular, Medium, Semibold, Bold.
Available Nunito Sans weights: Light, Regular, SemiBold, Bold, ExtraBold.

rules.intent: Enforce the approved font roles and weights across mediums.
rules.rule_text: Typography roles by medium: headlines/headers use Agenda in print (font.headline.print = Agenda) and Nunito Sans in digital/email (Title Case, bolder weights), with Aptos as the Word/PPT/Excel fallback. Body copy, footers and fine print use Nunito Sans (Aptos fallback). Agenda may be used as live text in digital for the campaign headline ONLY. Available Agenda weights: Light, Regular, Medium, Semibold, Bold; Nunito Sans weights: Light, Regular, SemiBold, Bold, ExtraBold; Aptos weights: Regular, Bold.

quotes: `'Nunito Sans', Verdana, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif`
**[GENERAL] Email font stack (CSS):** `'Nunito Sans', Verdana, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif`. Nunito Sans must be loaded via `@font-face`/Google Fonts link where the client supports it

rules.intent: Ensure consistent web-font rendering with graceful fallback in email.
rules.rule_text: [GENERAL] The email CSS font stack is `'Nunito Sans', Verdana, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif` (font.email.css_stack). Nunito Sans must be loaded via @font-face/Google Fonts link where the client supports it; the stack guarantees graceful fallback in Outlook and other clients that strip webfonts.

quotes: 'Nunito Sans', Verdana, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif
H1 (section hero headline): 28px / 36px
H2 (section heading): 22px / 30px
H3 (subhead): 18px / 26px
Body: 16px / 24px (150%)
16px is the brand-mandated minimum for body copy ("bigger is better")
use sparingly, never for primary content
must be "noticeably smaller" than primary data and clearly separated from it
Never set running text below 12px. (ISI sizing is owned by the locked component.)

rules.intent: Maintain a consistent, accessible type hierarchy and minimum sizes in email.
rules.rule_text: [GENERAL] Email type scale (px / line-height): H1 section hero headline 28/36 (~130%) Nunito Sans ExtraBold/Bold; H2 section heading 22/30 (~136%) Bold; H3 subhead 18/26 (~144%) Bold/SemiBold; Body 16/24 (150%) Regular — 16px (size.body.minimum_16) is the brand-mandated minimum for body copy ("bigger is better"); Secondary/supporting body 14/21 (150%) Regular, use sparingly, never for primary content; Footnote/reference text within a section 12/18 (150%), must be "noticeably smaller" than primary data and clearly separated from it. Never set running text below 12px (size.text.min_running = 12); ISI sizing is owned by the locked component.

quotes: Line height: minimum 150% for all body copy
**Line height: minimum 150% for all body copy** (brand accessibility rule). **For light text on dark backgrounds, increase further** — [GENERAL] use 160% (e.g., 16px/26px).

rules.intent: Preserve legibility with adequate line spacing, more on dark backgrounds.
rules.rule_text: Body line height must be a minimum of 150% for all body copy (brand accessibility rule). For light text on dark backgrounds, increase further — [GENERAL] use 160% (e.g., 16px/26px).

quotes: Use a clear **type scale / visual hierarchy** so the reader knows what to read first, next, last. Never use two adjacent text blocks at the same size+weight to signal different hierarchy levels.

rules.intent: Ensure hierarchy is legibly expressed through distinct type treatments.
rules.rule_text: Use a clear type scale / visual hierarchy so the reader knows what to read first, next, last. Never use two adjacent text blocks at the same size+weight to signal different hierarchy levels.
